2a68a088-8f35-4791-9917-1119cdb845fc.name: Text
2a68a088-8f35-4791-9917-1119cdb845fc.text:
Clause 2. Loan Withdrawal or Disbursement  
2.1. Party B may withdraw or request for disbursement of the Loan if Party B has fulfilled all obligations and conditions, precedent and subsequent, as stated in Annex 1 of this Loan for Consumption Contract.  
2.2. Party A will disburse to Party B the Loan in cash or through wire transfer into Party B’s account maintained with Party A unless otherwise stated in Annex 1 of this Loan for Consumption Contract.  
Clause 3. Interest and Expenses  
3.1. Interest: Party B agrees to pay interest in accordance with this Loan for Consumption Contract at a rate as set out in Clause 1 on the basis of daily interest calculation over the outstanding principal amount of the Loan.  
3.2. Default Interest Rate: In case where Party B fails to pay or to pay on time any amount due, Party B shall pay default interest at the rate of [27] % ([twenty seven] percent) per annum over the overdue amount in addition to the Fixed Interest Rate set out in Clause 1 above.  
3.3. Party B shall be responsible for other expenses including: □ lawyer fee for contract preparation of [-], □ collateral valuation cost of [-], security registration cost of [-] and security deregistration cost of [-], that Party B shall pay to relevant stakeholders.  
Clause 4. Repayment of Loan  
4.1. Party B shall repay the Loan in accordance with the amount and regularly at the dates as set out in the repayment schedule provided by Party A.

db5ec18d-378e-427f-9cd7-dc4cf3d6a639.name: Text
db5ec18d-378e-427f-9cd7-dc4cf3d6a639.text:
In case where Party B prepays the Loan in whole or in part during the lock-in period, being a period of up to half(1/2) of the entire Loan Tenor which however shall not exceed a maximum period of 24 months from the date of the initial loan disbursement (the “Lock-in Period”), Party B shall:  
    (i) inform the Bank/Institution in writing at least 30 calendar days in advance;  
    (ii) pay early settlement expense in an amount equal to 3% of the principal loan amount being prepaid.  
b. In case where Party B prepays the Loan in whole or in part after the Lock-in Period, Party B shall inform the Bank/Institution in writing at least 30 calendar days in advance. In case where Party B fails to inform as required, Party B shall pay early settlement expense in an amount equal to 1% of the principal loan amount being prepaid.

b5ba3358-7f51-4707-9a99-60e252c153e0.text:
Clause 5. Events of Default 
5.1. An Event of Default means a breach of contract or a failure to perform any obligation by Party B and/or the security provider/guarantor in accordance with this Loan for Consumption Contract, including any of the following events:

runs.text: 5.1. An Event of Default means a breach of contract or a failure to perform any obligation by Party B and/or the security provider/guarantor in accordance with this Loan for Consumption Contract, including any of the following events:

29588734-495f-4d10-a561-8db4f8aee0e2.name: Text
29588734-495f-4d10-a561-8db4f8aee0e2.text:
a. The Credit is not used for the purpose as stated in Clause 1 of this Loan for Consumption Contract.  
b. The use of Credit by Party B causes adverse environmental and social impact or the Credit is used to support any business activity restricted by laws.  
c. Party B fails to repay the principal, interest, default interest, and/or any expense as stated in this Loan for Consumption Contract at the respective due date.  
d. Party B is convicted of a criminal offense (under court judgement).

8b247991-9690-424b-98de-2e288bfb94cb.name: Text
8b247991-9690-424b-98de-2e288bfb94cb.text:
a. Party A may in its discretion require that the Loan become immediately due and be repaid fully or partially together with accrued interest thereon and all other amounts payable under this Loan for Consumption Contract. If Party B fails to pay or to pay on-time any amount due, Party B shall pay default interest at the default interest rate as set out in Clause 3.2. over such overdue amount in addition to the Fixed Interest Rate set out in Clause 1 above until Party A receives full settlement.  
b. In case where Party B fails to pay or to pay on-time any amount due, Party A has the right to attend to meet with Party B at Party B’s address or business location or at any other arrangeable place to identify any appropriate resolution.  
c. Party A has the right to exercise all or any of the remedies, including through compulsory performance, pursuant to this Loan for Consumption Contract or as allowed by laws either concurrently or consecutively. No delay in exercising any such remedies shall be considered as any waiver of Party A’s right to do so.

389c5e07-4bc0-4088-997b-b3fc3d4733fd.name: Text
389c5e07-4bc0-4088-997b-b3fc3d4733fd.text:
Clause 6. Miscellaneous Provisions  
6.1. Adherence to Environmental, Social and Governance Principles and Clients Protection Principles  
Party B shall adhere to the Environmental, Social and Governance Principles and the Clients Protection Principles as stated in Annex 2 of this Loan for Consumption Contract.  
6.2. Provision and Disclosure of Information  
Party B shall provide and update relevant information and supporting documents with respect to the loan transaction as required by Party A and, so long as the Loan remains in place and outstanding or where required by laws, Party B also hereby agrees and allows that Party A may declare or disclose all or any of such data or information, including that related to existing or future accounts and reports or documents regularly provided in relation to the Loan or the business of Party B to the National Bank of Cambodia, the Credit Bureau (Cambodia), auditors, provisional administrators/liquidators or other relevant authorities or to Party A’s related entities, partners, or agents, or in accordance with court order, legal action, suit or proceedings relating to the Loan.

83bbaedd-bdde-4a19-b1cb-d1fb451e456f.name: Text
83bbaedd-bdde-4a19-b1cb-d1fb451e456f.text:
a. This Loan for Consumption Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with, the laws of the Kingdom of Cambodia.  
b. In case of any objection or complaint in relation to the Credit/Loan herein, Party B may contact the [Bank/Institution] via customer complaint handling telephone No.: 023 999 989 / 012 999 489) or the customer complaint handling team of the Association of Banks in Cambodia via telephone No.: (023 238 760/092 771 881) or of the Cambodia Microfinance Association via telephone No.: (015 365 222).  
c. The Parties shall endeavor to settle any disagreements or differences relating to the interpretation and implementation of this Loan for Consumption Contract in an amicable manner to reach an out-of-court resolution. In any case where an amicable resolution cannot be reached, any Party may refer the dispute to the competent court of the Kingdom of Cambodia for resolution. All expenses of dispute settlement in any legal proceedings shall be on the account of the breaching party.

After receiving the agreement to guarantee the loan from the Credit Guarantee Corporation of
Cambodia with the issued Letter of Guarantee No.: ……………………………………… dated:
………………………………………, Party “B” has agreed to pay the following fees:
- Annual Credit Guarantee fee: ……………. percent over the Guaranteed Amount. Party “B” shall pay in
cash when signing the contract or authorizes Party “A” to debit from Party “B”’s account following
entering into the contract and shall pay annually against the outstanding Guaranteed Amount after
receiving the notice from Party “A” based on actual the actual formal invoice or document and shall
settle no longer than the 20th day of each month. In the case where the 20th day falls on a holiday or
national public holiday, the settlement shall be made before the 20th day or at the last working day
before such holiday.
- Credit guarantee fee at 0.5% (zero-point five percent) of the outstanding guaranteed loan in case Party
“B” prepays the loan in full to Party “A” before the maturity date of the Loan Contract.
- Additional credit guarantee fee at 0.5% (zero-point five percent) of the outstanding guaranteed amount
in case Party “B has not duly fulfilled the relevant registration obligation within 01 (one) year after
receiving the guarantee from the Credit Guarantee Corporation of Cambodia.

accordance with the following:

-   Business activities involving landfilling of natural lakes, river, waterways, or hydropower construction
     that causes flooding, drought, and the loss of biodiversity.
-   Production or trade in pharmaceuticals and hazardous chemicals subject to bans by national laws and
     international conventions or agreements.
-   Business activities involving the production or generation of significant amount of solid and
     liquid/water waste.
-    Business activities involving the production or emission of dust, smoke, odor, disturbing noise
     that cause adverse impact on the daily lives of people in the surrounding community.

d5cae7bd-52e0-4f0e-9806-d7fd9cccaeb2.name: Text
d5cae7bd-52e0-4f0e-9806-d7fd9cccaeb2.text:
-    Production or business activities involving harmful or exploitative forms of forced labor or of child
labor.
-    Production or trade in weapons and munitions.
-    Gambling, casinos, prostitution and equivalent enterprises.
-    Trade in wildlife or wildlife product regulated under the law.
-    Production or trade in radioactive materials. This does not apply to the purchase of medical equipment,
quality control (measurement) equipment and any equipment where the Bank/Institution considers the
radioactive source to be trivial and/or adequately shielded.
-    Production or trade in any product or activity deemed illegal under the laws or regulations of the
Kingdom of Cambodia or international conventions and agreements, or subject to international bans,
such as pesticides/herbicides, ozone depleting substances, PCB’s, wildlife or products regulated under
CITES.
-    Production and trade in any product subject to international reduction or bans of which the
Bank/Institution has been officially aware of.
-    Production or service, which cause recognized and serious damage to the natural environment and are
forbidden under the laws of the Kingdom of Cambodia.
-    Any other activities deemed illegal under the laws or regulations of the Kingdom of Cambodia or
international conventions and agreements.
-    Production or trade in wood or other forestry products other than from sustainably managed forests.
-    Production or trade in tobacco.
-    Production or trade in alcoholic beverages (excluding beer and wine).
-    Production or trade in unbonded asbestos fibers. This does not apply to purchase and use of bonded
asbestos cement sheeting where the asbestos content is less than 20%.
-    Commercial logging operations for use in primary tropical moist forest.
-    Production, trade, storage, or transport of significant volumes of hazardous chemicals, or commercial
scale usage of hazardous chemicals. Hazardous chemicals include gasoline, kerosene, and other
petroleum products.
-    Production or trade in pharmaceuticals subject to international phase outs or bans.
-    Production or trade in pesticides/herbicides subject to international phase outs or bans.
-    Production or trade in ozone depleting substances subject to international phase outs or bans.
-    Drift net fishing in the marine environment using nets in excess of 2.5km in length.
-    Production or activities that impinge on the lands owned, or claimed under adjudication, by indigenous
peoples or non-indigenous peoples, without full documented consent of such peoples.
-    Production or distribution of anti-democracy or racial discrimination, information.

f7269970-122c-45a6-92a4-049a79f273f7.name: Text
f7269970-122c-45a6-92a4-049a79f273f7.text:
The Bank/Institution is highly committed to implementing its ethical code of conduct and strictly maintaining the triple bottom line (people, planet, and profit) as well as respecting the environment,
society, and community we are in. Therefore, the following seven client protection principles will be duly adhered to:
(1) Appropriate product design and delivery
(2) Prevention of over-indebtedness
(3) Transparency
(4) Responsible pricing
(5) Fair and respectful treatment of clients
(6) Privacy of client data
(7) Mechanisms for complaint resolution

bbacfbba-f48f-4aa4-b6a8-3f67e682c834.name: Text
bbacfbba-f48f-4aa4-b6a8-3f67e682c834.text:
Bank refers to Wing Bank (Cambodia) Plc., a commercial bank registered under the laws of Cambodia including its
registered branch offices (“Party A”).
 
Loan Applicant refers to any person who applies for WING DIGITAL LOAN through Wing Bank App. and Wing
Agent, and voluntarily go through the loan application process until before it is rejected or successfully granted the
Loan (“You” or “Party B”).
 
Loan for Consumption Contract refers to a Loan for Consumption Contract between you and the Bank for the
provision of WING DIGITAL LOAN. The Loan for Consumption Contract is attached above.
 
Royal Group of Companies refers to companies which are affiliated and listed as under Royal Group of Companies
and the list of companies may be expanded or changed from time to time (“RGC”).
 
RGC Salary Loan refer to a personal loan offered by Wing Bank to employees of companies which are affiliated and
listed under RGC. The Credit limitation can be up to USD 10,000. It is very convenient and quick way through digital
application process without collateral or guarantor required, and cash in directly to Wing Bank Account of
customer (“WING DIGITAL LOAN”).
 
Primary Information refers to the information and documents provided by the Loan Applicant to the Bank through
Wing Bank App. and Wing Agent for the purpose of assessing the credit limitation, which information includes
without limitation to full name, gender, date of birth, place of birth, residential address, occupation, financial
statement and other supporting documents.
 
Wing Agent refers to an independent agent who is appointed and authorized to exclusively performs a range of
services offered by the Bank to the Bank’s customers including processing WING DIGITAL LOAN application.
 
Bank Account refers to bank account(s) that the Loan Applicant has the possession on including payroll account
and other accounts the Loan Applicant opened at the Bank.
 
Wing Bank App. refers to an application on a mobile phone owned by the Bank which is the tool used by the Loan
Applicant for loan request.

shall fully pay off the Loan including but not limited to principal, interest, and penalty charged (if any). In this case,
you consent the Bank to freeze the outstanding balance of your payroll or other accounts maintained with the Bank for payingoff the Loan. If there is no balance or insufficient balance, you shall deposit extra money to fully
pay off the loan within 5 (five) working days before your last day.

03baee55-e8d6-4f90-951d-fdf220af1fe6.name: Text
03baee55-e8d6-4f90-951d-fdf220af1fe6.text: Any modification or amendment to this Loan for Consumption Contract shall only be valid if made in writing with signatures/thumbprints of the Parties.

runs.text: Any modification or amendment to this Loan for Consumption Contract shall only be valid if made in writing with signatures/thumbprints of the Parties.

3a30a86c-8566-4c43-89cf-ba031b94b758.name: Text
3a30a86c-8566-4c43-89cf-ba031b94b758.text: This Loan for Consumption Contract shall be binding upon the successors-in-title and assigns of Party A and the heirs and the successors-in-title of Party B.

runs.text: This Loan for Consumption Contract shall be binding upon the successors-in-title and assigns of Party A and the heirs and the successors-in-title of Party B.

d3c5fd80-cf3c-49df-9fa7-8ca236baa591.name: Text
d3c5fd80-cf3c-49df-9fa7-8ca236baa591.text: The repayment schedules, the security/guaranty contract, annexes, and other documents related to the Loan/Credit shall form part of this Loan for Consumption Contract as entire agreement.

runs.text: The repayment schedules, the security/guaranty contract, annexes, and other documents related to the Loan/Credit shall form part of this Loan for Consumption Contract as entire agreement.
